name: Makit
desc: Satisfyingly smooth-skinned, porous mushrooms that absorb any memories they hear or overhear

name: Wallflower
desc: Timid forest creatures with peony petals for curls and softly whistling wind for breath

name: Emoorah
desc: Sentient, beautiful swans who dread water and long for a mate they suspect doesn't exist

name: Treant
desc: Living trees that travel the world debating philosophy, raising children or building furniture

name: Aspidochiron
desc: Mountain-sized tortoises whose shells are lined with ladders and forests of food-bearing plants

name: Saketachew
desc: A rain spirit, rainfall falls as colored, spherical resin that sticks to targets

name: Baku
desc: Dream-eating tapirs that grant prophetic visions to whoever lies with them

name: Moonlight dragon
desc: Serpentine creatures that sculpt spans from trunk-like appendages and retract their wings into their necks. They sleep beneath ice-caves glimmering with minuscule, cold luminous crystals

name: Unicorn
desc: Evanescent equines who wander through fairy forests and heal the barren with tears of panacea

name: Yi Ni
desc: Invisible tricksters that disassemble assembled objects and reassemble disassembled objects, who carry fissures in their pockets

name: Xerephs
desc: Ever-sad creatures that shed watermelons as tears, often leading brigands to emerald mines

name: Odonsaii
desc: Hyper-intelligent invertebrates that can communicate telepathically through their shimmering blue, radulae-filled maws

name: Squishmeat
desc: Annoying and adorable creatures, their bodies are filled entirely of cheek, with eyestalks and stumpy little appendages

name: Whooly
desc: Excitable, pet-like creatures that carry various silken eggs, which hatch into shellgems in sunlight

name: Striped Snurggle
desc: Eel-like creatures that shoot stunning jolts of electricity at prospective mates to impress them

name: Grimoire
desc: Spellbooks haunted by or made up entirely of irritable magical beings

name: Tsuredure
desc: Small, elegant, humanoid bird, beloved for their cheeky ability to change every orb they touch into exactly the right kind of feather

name: Hex Pig
desc: Efficient, adorable animals who dash across mountainsides like jackrabbits with silver buckeyes for eyes and ruby snouts

name: Mundane
desc: Relatively unremarkable creatures

name: The Lone Local
desc: The local, except there is only one

name: Brownie
desc: Small, elfin humanoids who polish the bowls of kitchen utensils with cats' teeth

name: Ogre
desc: Hulking humanoid brutes that roar at loud insects and eat massive meals at diners

name: Sabertooth
desc: Cats with fangs longer than their tiny brains

name: Banolion
desc: Bristly-haired beasts that somehow appear to be wearing matching scarves

name: Kitawa
desc: Small monkeys with severe acne, known for clinging to travellers and demanding gaudy hats

name: Ginormous chicken
desc: A scaled, one-eyed bad-butt capable of defeating Cerberus. A small child sits on its back

name: Manbearpig
desc: A bear with a man face and the body of a pig

name: Deva
desc: Perfect immortal beings that are total jerks when you look at them in unhealthy ways

name: Eyegore
desc: Tentacled horrors that devour souls and fling invisible objects around rooms

name: Locho
desc: Small, grotesque, fist-sized horrors that fly through the air and burrow into the skulls of sleeping victims, causing remorse and visions of what could have been

name: Horrificasculus
desc: The legless, and the eyeless, and the smelling of the skin of a flayed corpse

name: Embryoth
desc: Giant maggots that dissolve their victims first with their rancid breath before eating the remains

name: Hollow
desc: Visored warriors that have shapeshifted into flawless replicas of their enemies

name: Nuckelavee
desc: Centaurian horse-beings with the ability to dissolve into a burst of lurid slime and pull victims in

name: Charrhawk
desc: Glowing-eyed, bone-faced, nightmare creatures that hunt in packs for their pack alpha

name: Thousand Pox
desc: Itching, yellow, foot-high lumps that crawl across the countryside leaving a path of diseased scabs

name: Charybdis
desc: Cyclopean whirlpools with seismic throats that swallow fleets of ships and belch them up hours later

name: Quinom'
desc: A dragon that eats sunlight and hides within the clouds, then leaps forth and devours a mountain

name: Nidhogg
desc: Dining on corpses on the world-tree Yggrasil, this ravenous wyrm gnaws at the foundation of reality

name: Bahamut
desc: Mightiest of dragons, with wings spanning mountains and scales glinting like polished silver

name: Kraken
desc: Monstrous cephalopods with tentacles like hundred-meter chainsaws

name: Couatl
desc: Vast rainbow serpents with wings of iridescent pheasant tail feathers, native to the middle world

name: Nightmare
desc: Ravenous terrors that appear as great ravenous maws with feathery bone wings and vulture feet

name: Dethelfin
desc: Shimmering, purple dolphins that gallop through ocean tides and drown men to exact vengeance upon Hades itself

name: Mnemosyne
desc: A humanoid embodiment of memory; devours souls and in return shares the memories

name: Mimir
desc: Developed from the heads of the valiantly slain, these orbs possess programs of wit and counsels of wisdom

name: Ouroboronion
desc: Snakes shaped into strange loops that swim through cosmic dimensions and emit great bursts of esoteric knowledge

name: Lyrebird
desc: Dancing songbirds with the ability to duplicate any sound, including entire conversations

name: Dresden
desc: A.k.a Cat, the nightmare cat who devours souls through the power of smug

name: Anosy
desc: Devoid of all physical form, except an infamously non-descript, angelic frame
